transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hey, Jason.
Hey, Bea.
How you doing?
Good.
How are you doing?
Pretty good.
You getting ready for some time off?
I am.
I'm actually going to the Galapagos.
I don't know if I mentioned.
No way.
Yeah, so we've got a big trip coming up.
Wow.
When do you leave?
Saturday.
Cool.
Okay, so you go to what?
Ecuador?
Yeah, so we'll go to Quito, spend a few days there, and from there, you kind of fly down to, I can't even remember what island it is.
I think there's one island that you can fly to and catch your boat.
Very cool.
And then how long will you be in Galapagos?
It'll be like a two-week trip, so it's a big one.
Wow.
Oh, that's awesome.
Yeah, we're like traveling around, everything, between the travel, mean, it feels like it takes like two days to get there.
Yeah, I bet.
And then we'll be on the boat for, I think, seven nights, eight days, and then like a couple days around Quito.
And stuff.
So, yeah, we've been planning the trip for a year.
My parents are coming.
My kids are coming.
It's going to be a big one.
Wow.
Have you been, like, reading books and, like, learning all about it and all that stuff?
I mean, I would.
I should be.
I gave my daughter, my nine-year-old, a book on animals at the Galapagos for Christmas.
So I'm hoping she'll just read it and then tell me what I'm looking at.
Totally.
Yeah.
You want to know what you're looking at and all that.
I know.
So, yeah, it's very exciting.
Hopefully, next time you see me, I'll have some, you know, selfies with baby sea lions to share.
Cool.
Very cool.
All right.
So let me share our agenda.
Agendas, they feel like my externalized brain at this point.
Thanks.
So, yeah, I guess one question, you sort of, I don't know if you had a chance to, or if Michael had a chance to take a look at the reporting, and I didn't know if you all had any questions, kind of.
Bye.
Coming out of that.
I did.
I myself don't have any questions.
And I don't know.
I did not follow up with Michael to see if he had reviewed.
I assume that he looked at them.
He usually is pretty good about that stuff.
And just the fact that I didn't hear anything tells me that he probably doesn't have any questions.
But yeah.
And, you know, next time we have that kind of review, I will make a little more effort to make sure that he's coming because that would have been nice to have him there.
But yeah.
Yeah.
Awesome.
Well, just wanted to check in because I know there were sort of some recommendations that came out of it.
So if there's anything you want to kind of circle back, you know, post-holiday, anything we can support in, let us know.
Yeah.

Because I imagine there's some things like if you want to add inline CTAs and stuff, that's some stuff that would probably make sense to loop in our dev team because they can kind of help coordinate with some of that stuff.
Yeah.
And, you know, that one, I think I've got.
I a pretty clear idea of how to do that, the first two.
Yeah, I think those are pretty straightforward and kind of on my shoulders to do that.
Honestly, probably won't happen until beginning of next year.
But yeah, I like the idea of doing those things.
Yeah, awesome.
And then, so we did check in on the FAQ schema, it looks like it's all working on our end.
So right now, we're adding it manually, but I think as we speak, they're trying to link up an auto-publishing flow that would automatically add that.
So I may come back, if they hit any roadblocks or have any questions, I kind of may come back with it on Slack.
But the goal is to get that all set up so that we can essentially just hit play straight from the pipeline and get it all ported in with the schema and everything intact.
That would be great.
I think, thank you.
you.

For your patience on the last round of content and also on catching those errors, the background is we had like a technical issue with your pipeline and had to kind of copy it and create a new one and make some edits.
So I think there were just a couple things.
I think the information about the tables lived inside my brain and not inside the system.
So it's now living in the system itself.
So thank you for catching those.
Yeah, yeah.

And then the other thing, the capability thing, just about the flight status monitoring, you know, I was seeing that and was like, oh, you know, but then I looked in Notion and it was, we did, you know, it's on us, but we had it in there wrong about, you know, it literally was in the here's what auto does and it was like, whatever.
So I was like, okay, , like that's why it's happening.
So I updated that.
Well, I think that was something that we like just verbally also had gone back and forth on how to position that in articles.
So I also went back and had our writers just check to make sure because, you know, that.
Lots of reappears across artifacts to make sure that it's not contradicting itself.
okay, perfect.
So we should be good to go there.
That shouldn't crop up again.
Okay.

I think those were the, all of the things, you know, I think, so just so you know, I plan to send an async update tomorrow, just so you know, kind of what content is coming, when it's coming, when to expect it.

And, you know, if I'm out, like who you can, who's your contact person, we'll have someone monitoring the Slack channel, but just so you know, who to, you might expect answers from, just so you'll sort of know what's happening on those weeks that we're not meeting.
Okay.
Sounds good.
You know, and I'm, I'm super flexible too.
I don't have like particular targets for like this week, next week, whatever, like if it, if it stretches out or, you know, we have more the beginning of the year versus the end, like not.
Concerned about that.
So whatever you guys want to.
Well, I appreciate your flexibility there.
But yeah, that's good to know.
And I think it's just good to have it a living document somewhere.
So we sort of know what we what we owe you.
But it's nice to know that there's a little bit of flexibility on the deadline.
Everyone shifts around their time off.
Yeah.
And, you know, I'll be around, but it's kind of like probably Monday, Tuesday of next week, and then maybe Monday, Tuesday of the week following.
you know, checking in on stuff, but everything's going to slow down, of course.
Yeah, I imagine that you also probably are not necessarily going to be reviewing blog posts on Christmas Day.
So not on Christmas Day.
And then just I'm thinking ahead to January.
We'll sort of do another round of reporting, kind of taking a look at performance, you know, metrics, checking to make sure that kind of sitemap is functioning as it's.
I imagine we're not going to have a ton of different recommendations.
That will probably be imaginable to be more of like a quarterly recommendation as we look and see what's performing.
But we'll plan to have that again in the first couple weeks of January.
And then one thing we're also touching base with clients on is just wanting to kind of stay up to date with your 2026 goals and planning.

So as you have those sessions internally, it's often great for us to kind of dedicate one of our meetings to just filling us in, letting us know what your goals are and kind of how we can support those.

Because sometimes it means a little bit of a strategy refresh or sometimes, you know, if you have new products launching, it's all just kind of good to sort of set the time to stay up to date with that.
Yeah, that's that's good.
That that sounds like that'll be a good conversation because, yeah, in my mind, like our product hasn't changed a lot, but we do have more like planning.
Capabilities for complex itineraries, like I feel like there's maybe some topics that we could kind of include around that.
And then we're starting to put energy towards a pilot partnership.
We have a company that is going to start using us for like 20 of their employees.

And I just wonder if there's, you know, not necessarily writing about that specifically, but like that audience perhaps, like are we for next year going to maybe want to try to use some of our content?
So reach those folks who might consider using us for a pilot or, you know, for their company.
Yeah.
Are you, I'm just thinking for that pilot program, are you sort of planning to collect data or like are there ways you're measuring the success of that partnership?
I think it's going to be mainly like qualitative, you know, because it's a small number of people, you know, it's a very small percentage of their actual organization, right?
Right.
So it's very much like a hand-holding kind of exercise and like.
All right, let's, we're going be talking with them to get user feedback and, you know, closely following their activity.
Are they using it or not?
But it'll be pretty hands-on, not like a, it's not going to be like an analytics exercise.
going to be more qualitative.
I mean, I think there's going to be a lot of ways to use something like that.
I mean, I think targeting other companies that might want to do something similar for sure.

If you end up producing some kind of, I would think like a case study, which is probably something more that feels like less automated, but something that you might create on your blog, then that could definitely be something, you know, we prioritize linking to and maybe put some thought into key or like more strategy and research around that company level person, because that's not someone we're targeting right now.
So I think it may be mean adding an extra persona and sort of rethinking a new topic cluster targeting that person, and then linking out to that case study to kind of.
Show the results.
Yeah, I like that.
And, you know, we're probably some time away from that, but just something, you know, since you teed up next year, it's something that's on my mind.
So no urgency there, but yeah, we can kind of sync up on that.
Yeah, well, I think that's why it's great to have these conversations.
Yeah, so we know what's happening and we can sort of start thinking on our end too.
Because, yeah, that's, to me, seems like that would be like a great opportunity to just expand the strategy a little bit, to include that persona once you have the results of that.
And that's something we can kind of point to.
Yeah, okay, cool.
Awesome.
All right, well, that is, that's it from me for now.
Okay, awesome.
Well, enjoy your trip.
Thank you.
Yeah, and happy holidays.
Enjoy your holidays as well.
Yeah, have good one.
All right, talk to you later.
Okay, bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on reporting, content fixes, and 2026 strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=99.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Reporting Review:** Jason reviewed the latest performance report and will implement inline CTAs and other recommendations in early 2026.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=110.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline Fix:** A technical issue that caused recent content errors (e.g., missing tables) is resolved. An auto-publishing flow for FAQ schema is in development to prevent future manual errors.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)
  - [**2026 Strategy:** A new B2B pilot partnership (20 employees) creates an opportunity to expand the content strategy by targeting a new corporate persona with a dedicated topic cluster and a future case study.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
  - [**Holiday Coverage:** Bailey is out starting Dec 20. An async update will be sent Dec 19 detailing the holiday content schedule and the primary contact for support.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)

## Topics

### Reporting Review & Recommendations

  - [Jason confirmed he reviewed the performance report and has no questions.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=110.0)
  - [Michael's review status is unknown, but Jason will ensure his attendance at the next review.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=110.0)
  - [Jason will implement the report's recommendations (e.g., inline CTAs) in early 2026.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=149.0)
  - [GrowthX's dev team is available to assist with technical implementations.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=149.0)

### Content Pipeline & Quality

  - [**Problem:** A technical issue during a pipeline migration caused recent content errors, including missing tables and incorrect feature descriptions.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)
  - [**Resolution:**](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)
      - [The pipeline is now fixed, with all necessary information stored in the system.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)
      - [Jason corrected the "flight status monitoring" description in Notion, which was the source of the error.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=266.0)
      - [GrowthX writers have updated all relevant artifacts to ensure consistency.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=288.0)
  - [**FAQ Schema Automation:**](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=199.0)
      - [**Current:** FAQ schema is added manually.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=199.0)
      - [**Goal:** Develop an auto-publishing flow to add schema automatically from the content pipeline, eliminating manual errors.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=199.0)

### 2026 Strategy & Goals

  - [**Context:** GrowthX requested an update on Otto's 2026 goals to align the content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=435.0)
  - [**New Opportunity:** Otto is launching a B2B pilot partnership with a company for 20 employees.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
      - [**Focus:** Qualitative user feedback, not quantitative analytics.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)
  - [**Strategic Response:** This partnership creates an opportunity to expand the content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
      - [**Action:** Develop a new topic cluster targeting a corporate persona.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=556.0)
      - [**Future Asset:** Create a case study from the pilot's results to serve as a key marketing asset.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=556.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Jason:** Implement reporting recommendations (e.g., inline CTAs) in early 2026.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=149.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)
      - [Send an async update (Dec 19) detailing the holiday content schedule and primary contact.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)
      - [Schedule the next performance review for early January.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=403.0)
      - [Begin planning a content strategy for the new corporate persona.](https://fathom.video/share/CLxx4BPrrx4kLFNhf82eXXKhktMN3fCG?tab=summary&timestamp=556.0)

action_items.description: Add Michael to next reporting review invite
Send async update to Jason re: content pipeline, delivery dates, holiday coverage contact
Schedule early Jan reporting review w/ Jason; include sitemap check
